channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Atwoli Urges IEBC to Control Early Political Rallies and Protect Workers
item.description: During the 61st Labour Day celebrations in Vihiga County, COTU Secretary General Francis Atwoli issued a strong call to the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) to clamp down on prem...

item.title: US Issues Sanctions Warning to Shipping Companies Paying Tolls to Iran in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States has issued a fresh warning to international shipping companies, stating that they could face sanctions if they make payments to Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Th...

item.title: Granville Street SRO set for closure as residents relocate to improved housing
item.description: The Luugat single-room occupancy (SRO) building on Vancouver’s Granville Street, purchased by the province for $55 million and converted to supportive housing during the COVID-19 pandemic, is set to c...

item.title: Uasin Gishu Couple Receives Car Gift from Children in Australia During Thanksgiving
item.description: Ishmael Kiptoo and his wife Judith Kiptoo from Maji Mingi in Uswo, Uasin Gishu County were overjoyed after their children living in Australia surprised them with a brand new car. The gift came during ...

item.title: Trump Advocates Cognitive Testing for Presidential Candidates
item.description: On May 1, 2026, former President Donald Trump addressed residents of The Villages, a large retirement community in Florida, emphasizing his belief that candidates for president and vice president shou...

item.title: Pakistan inflation rises to nearly 11% in April amid surge in energy and transport costs
item.description: Pakistan’s inflation has once again entered double digits after 21 months, reaching close to 11% year-on-year in April, according to official data. This increase has mainly been driven by sharp rises ...

item.title: Government Acknowledges the Role of Workers on Workers' Day 2026
item.description: On 1 May 2026, South Africa observed Workers' Day, a public holiday dedicated to honouring the contributions of workers across all sectors. The Acting Director-General of the Government Communication ...

item.title: 19-year-old arrested after Peter Kay Birmingham show evacuated over suspicious bag
item.description: A 19-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of a bomb hoax offence following the evacuation of a Peter Kay performance at Birmingham's Utilita Arena. The incident occurred on Friday night during ...

item.title: Demographic shifts: living longer with fewer children and its societal impact
item.description: Many countries around the world are facing a significant demographic shift as deaths begin to outnumber births, driven by longer life expectancy and declining fertility rates. In nations like Japan, I...

item.title: UBJP still waiting for Comelec accreditation ahead of BARMM parliamentary elections
item.description: The United Bangsamoro Justice Party (UBJP) is still waiting for final accreditation from the Commission on Elections (Comelec) for the first-ever Bangsamoro Parliamentary Elections scheduled on Septem...

item.title: Update on Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni Legal Dispute Over 'It Ends With Us'
item.description: The high-profile legal battle between actors Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni, stemming from the production and promotion of the 2024 film 'It Ends With Us', continues to unfold in court. Lively initia...

item.title: BMW X3 Pure Design review: Comfortable entry-level luxury SUV built in South Africa
item.description: The BMW X3 Pure Design brings a refreshing take on the popular luxury SUV, offering a more elegant and comfortable alternative to the sportier M Sport variants. Launched initially without a pure petro...

item.title: Bukoba Municipality to Undergo 30.2bn/- Infrastructure Upgrade Under TACTIC Project
item.description: Bukoba Municipality in Kagera Region is on the path of significant transformation following the implementation of the 30.2bn/- Transforming Infrastructure and Competitiveness (TACTIC) project. Accordi...

item.title: Royal Family releases new photograph of Princess Charlotte to mark her 11th birthday
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales have released a new photograph of Princess Charlotte to celebrate her 11th birthday. Shared on their official social media accounts, the image shows Charlotte smiling ...

item.title: Linux Kernel Vulnerability CVE-2026-31431 (CopyFail) Enables Local Privilege Escalation Across Distributions
item.description: A newly disclosed vulnerability in the Linux kernel, dubbed CopyFail and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, allows unprivileged users to escalate their privileges to root on affected systems. Researchers from...

item.title: AWS Data Centers in the Middle East Remain Offline for Months Following Drone Damage
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is reportedly facing an extended recovery period after its cloud infrastructure in the Middle East suffered significant damage due to drone strikes. According to an AWS dashb...

item.title: Experts urge gradual phase-out of solar panel imports to protect energy access
item.description: Energy stakeholders and experts are advising the Nigerian government not to impose an outright ban on solar panel imports at this time. Instead, they are recommending a gradual phase-out approach that...

item.title: Tanzania Sets Up Carbon Trade Coordination Centre to Ensure Fair Payments to Communities
item.description: The Tanzanian government has launched a special Carbon Trade Coordination Centre to make sure local communities get fair benefits from carbon trading projects. Deputy Minister Festo Dugange told Parli...

item.title: Exploring Urrak Production at South Goa Cashew Plantations
item.description: During the months of April and May, the small Goan communities in Quepem and Canacona witness a unique local tradition centred around urrak, the lightly spiked precursor to Feni. The drink, which is m...

item.title: Comedian Njugush Revamps Matatu Tugi Following Separation from Wife Celestine Ndinda
item.description: Popular Kenyan comedian Njugush, whose real name is Timothy Kimani, has given his matatu named Tugi a major facelift. This comes shortly after he and his wife Celestine Ndinda confirmed their mutual s...
